openmeter/watermill/driver/kafka.purpose: Kafka transport driver for Watermill, building Sarama-backed publishers and subscribers from app config. Wraps watermill-kafka with OpenMeter-specific concerns: CloudEvent partition keys, SASL/SCRAM auth, Sarama logging redirection, topic provisioning, and OTel metrics via the metrics/ subpackage.

patterns.name: Options struct with Validate() then constructor
patterns.description: Each public constructor (NewPublisher, NewSubscriber) takes an Options struct embedding BrokerOptions and calls in.Validate() first thing, returning an error before building any Sarama/Watermill object.
patterns.example: func NewSubscriber(in SubscriberOptions) (message.Subscriber, error) { if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Centralized Sarama config via createKafkaConfig(role)
patterns.description: All Sarama config (SASL, TLS, metric registry, producer retries, logger) is built in BrokerOptions.createKafkaConfig(role) and handed to watermill via OverwriteSaramaConfig. role is 'publisher' or 'subscriber' and is required.

patterns.name: Injected dependencies, no global fallbacks
patterns.description: BrokerOptions requires a non-nil *slog.Logger and otelmetric.Meter; Validate() rejects nil. Logger is passed to watermill via watermill.NewSlogLogger and to the metrics registry via metrics.LoggingErrorHandler(o.Logger).
patterns.example: if o.Logger == nil { return errors.New("logger is required") }

patterns.name: CloudEvent subject becomes Kafka partition key
patterns.description: AddPartitionKeyFromSubject copies cloudEvent.Subject() into message metadata under PartitionKeyMetadataKey; marshalerWithPartitionKey then promotes that metadata to kafkaMsg.Key and strips the header. Publisher uses marshalerWithPartitionKey{} as Marshaler.

patterns.name: Sarama global logger redirected to slog adaptor
patterns.description: createKafkaConfig sets sarama.Logger and sarama.DebugLogger to SaramaLoggerAdaptor wrapping o.Logger.Info / .Debug. DebugLogger must always be set or debug logs fall back onto the info Logger.

patterns.name: Metric name transform via SaramaMetricRenamer(role)
patterns.description: Metrics are prefixed 'sarama.', tagged with a role attribute, and high-cardinality/low-value series (for-broker, for-topic, protocol-requests-rate, compression-) are dropped via TransformedMetric.Drop=true.

patterns.name: Topic provisioning before publisher returns
patterns.description: NewPublisher calls in.TopicProvisioner.Provision(ctx, in.ProvisionTopics...) before constructing the kafka.Publisher, so topics exist before the first publish.
patterns.example: if err = in.TopicProvisioner.Provision(ctx, in.ProvisionTopics...); err != nil { return nil, err }

key_file_guides.role: BrokerOptions + createKafkaConfig(role): the single place all Sarama config is assembled (SASL/SCRAM, TLS, metric registry, producer settings, loggers).
key_file_guides.watch_for: ApiVersionsRequest is deliberately false (log-flood workaround); TLS Config left as &tls.Config{} because Sarama breaks on forced TLS 1.3; producer MaxMessageBytes is 2MB. Don't reintroduce these as defaults blindly.

key_file_guides.role: NewPublisher: builds watermill kafka.Publisher with marshalerWithPartitionKey and the OTel Sarama tracer; provisions topics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: kafka.NewOTELSaramaTracer() relies on the GLOBAL trace provider, not an injected one.

key_file_guides.role: NewSubscriber: builds watermill kafka.Subscriber bound to a required ConsumerGroupName.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Consumer.MaxProcessingTime is hardcoded to 5m (defaultMaxProcessingTime) to avoid partition reassignment; uses DefaultMarshaler (no partition-key handling on the consume side).

key_file_guides.role: marshalerWithPartitionKey + AddPartitionKeyFromSubject: maps a CloudEvent subject to the Kafka message key.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PartitionKeyMetadataKey header is filtered out of the outgoing message after being promoted to Key; changing the key derivation changes partition assignment / ordering guarantees.

key_file_guides.role: SaramaMetricRenamer(role): returns the name-transform fn consumed by the metrics subpackage registry.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ingorePrefixes drops whole metric families; for-broker/for-topic are dropped to avoid high cardinality. New metrics must avoid matching these drop rules.

key_file_guides.role: XDGSCRAMClient (Begin/Step/Done) + SHA256/SHA512 hash generators, vendored from the Sarama example for SCRAM auth.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Wired only when SecurityProtocol == 'SASL_SSL' and SaslMechanisms is SCRAM-SHA-256/512 in createKafkaConfig.

key_file_guides.role: SaramaLoggerAdaptor: bridges Sarama's Print/Printf/Println logger interface to a single LoggerFunc.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Thin fmt.Sprint/Sprintf bridge; level (Info vs Debug) is chosen by which slog method is bound at construction in broker.go.

anti_patterns: Constructing Sarama config outside createKafkaConfig(role) — bypasses SASL/TLS/metrics/logger wiring and the role suffix on ClientID.
Using slog.Default() instead of the injected BrokerOptions.Logger; Validate() exists specifically to forbid nil loggers/meters.
Skipping in.Validate() in a new constructor before touching Sarama/Watermill objects.
Setting only sarama.Logger and not sarama.DebugLogger — debug output then floods the info logger.
Adding partition-key logic on the subscriber path; only the publisher uses marshalerWithPartitionKey, the subscriber uses DefaultMarshaler.

decisions.decision: OverwriteSaramaConfig injects a fully custom Sarama config into watermill-kafka rather than relying on watermill defaults.
decisions.rationale: OpenMeter needs control over SASL/SCRAM, TLS quirks, the OTel metric registry, producer message size, and Sarama's global loggers — none expressible through watermill's surface.

decisions.decision: Partition key is derived from the CloudEvent subject and carried through message metadata.
decisions.rationale: Guarantees per-subject (e.g. per-customer/meter) ordering and co-location across partitions for usage events.

decisions.decision: ApiVersionsRequest disabled and TLS left at library defaults instead of forcing 1.3.
decisions.rationale: Documented workarounds for known Sarama bugs (ApiVersionsRequest V3 log floods; TLS 1.3 'protocol version not supported').

code_examples.scenario: Build a Kafka publisher that partitions by CloudEvent subject
